frame_analyses.description: Needle holder is positioning to grip the suture needle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper grip technique observed, with focus on needle alignment.

frame_analyses.description: Needle tip is positioned at the first mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle inserted close to the marked point, minimal deviation expected.

frame_analyses.description: Surgeon's knot (double throw) being tied, first hand holding the initial loop.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The double throw is tight, decreasing the likelihood of slip.

frame_analyses.description: Single throw tied with switch of hand position.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch is smooth, throw appears tight and secure.

frame_analyses.description: Final single throw is being tied, second hand switch being performed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Throw executed well, ensuring knot security.

frame_analyses.description: Both ends of the suture are being cut close to the knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cut is clean and close, minimizing suture tail length.

knot_assessments.notes: Single throw tight and correctly performed after hand switch.
Final throw is well-executed, maintaining knot integrity.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No gap observed; drain remains securely attached.

technique_summary: The trainee effectively completed the task with good technique throughout, tight knot throws, and minimal deviation in suture placement.
improvement_suggestions: Work on further minimizing deviation in suture placement.
Practice achieving faster phase transitions to reduce time.
